Hey, all right, welcome back. So the first thing we want to do is we want to get you money. Or if you're working with a client, we want to get your clients some money. And generally speaking, like I said in the last video, almost every business I've ever worked with, if not literally, every business I've ever worked with, there is low hanging fruit available to them. And they just can't see it because they're so busy doing business owner stuff. Yeah, that's a technical term. I learned that at Harvard Business School, where I didn't go ever. But anyway, that's the deal. We're so busy that we can rarely see just massive glaring opportunities that are sitting right in front of us. Now, one of the most dangerous things we can do in our business, or if we're dealing with a client, is to simply go forth and say, you know, I'm going to start a project today. And what I want to do in this project is I want to get more money. The reason that's dangerous is because there's no quantifiable target and there's no quantifiable deadline. So when I'm working with clients and when we do this in our own business, so when I am my own client, I always use a process for uncovering immediate revenue that's called Sandor. And so what I want to do for you this week is I want to give you a crash course in some of the most important elements of Sandor. And the reason I want to do that is because we're going to end this week with you going out and actually bringing in revenue for your business based on what you learn how to uncover by going through this framework. So what I want to do is I want to take you to the computer and walk you through the overall steps of how the Sandor model works. And again, Sandor is a specific model of consulting that I use on my own business and for clients where we uncover what to do right now to get immediate revenue. So what I'm going to walk you through is the big picture of how Sandor works and then I'm going to show you how to do the very first step of the model. All right, so let's go and I'll show you how to do it. So here's the big picture overview of the Sandor model. Sandor, basically the first letter of each step spells out the word Sandor. To my knowledge, Sandor actually has no meaning. But the model itself is something. So our very first step is we get the stated objective or the target from our client. We also do this on a Focus Finder call, by the way. But remember, in our rules, we said, hey, we're always target and deadline driven. So this needs to always be me, the client now, I want to achieve X result by Y time. Okay, so our first mission is to get that and determine whether or not it's gettable. And not the stated objective itself is gettable, but whether or not we can actually hit that target. And the way we determine whether or not we can hit the target is through these next two steps. Second step is A, it stands for assets. Now assets just means all the stuff they've got going on in their business that could be used to hit this target, right? So this is like email lists, product lines, Facebook ads, campaign sales processes, stuff like that. We get it all dumped out on the table, and then we get the third step, which is the numbers. And that is, hey, how well did all of this stuff work? Now, you know what's funny is so many times you're going to have a client say, I want to hit X. And then you're going to through these two steps right here, determine that there was a campaign that worked really, really well. And the sales process that worked really, really well. And you're going to know because you got the numbers on it. And your prescription for them is going to be like, hey, do that again. And that's actually going to be the thing that moves the needle for them. But I'll let you experience that for yourself because I know it's kind of hard to believe. Once we have the numbers, which is just things like conversion rates, profit, how well campaigns did the usual stuff. And I'll walk you through how to get every single one of these. Don't worry. Now we do what's called a diagnosis. And this is where your diagnostic tools come in. And this is sometimes it's easy. Actually, ultimately, all of this becomes very, very easy the more you do it. But if at first it isn't easy, please don't worry because I've got tools for you based on business type and based on sales process type, what to look for. So this is pretty simple when you have the right tools, which you will because I'm giving them to you. But the diagnosis really lets us see, okay, what's the one thing that the client needs to do right now in order to move closer to this guy over here, which is the stated objective. Your final step is to review and repeat. And the reason why is because it's not like you do one thing and then their life changes forever. You do one thing and it works pretty good. And then we go through and we find another one thing until we hit this target. But believe it or not, the one thing is almost always, hey, that thing that used to work really good and you stop doing it, start doing it and keep doing it. And then let's add some more follow up and then let's add some additional offers during your sales process. Sort of got as almost always that, thankfully. Okay, but this is the process. This is our model, really simple model, very, very rare. And the thing that sets you apart is this part right here. All of your competition, if they ever bother to get any of this other data, all the other consultants out there, what they're going to do is they're going to give your client, or the client, seven billion things to do. And it's just going to overwhelm them some more. Our real value is in A, getting this, analyzing these to come up with the one thing to do right now, to get them the most powerful and impactful results in the shortest amount of time period. Right? And so we can do none of that stuff unless we get the stated objective. And that is what our mission for this week's training is. So let's talk about it. It starts obviously with a question. And as bizarre as this sounds, I'm actually going to tell you different ways to ask the question. And the reason why is because if you ever get a client that actually answers the question completely and specifically, it will be like a miracle. They almost never know the answer to this question. So we really have to work on getting the stated objective from them. So first method is the Dan Sullivan style. Right? And Dan Sullivan has this thing. It's called the R Factor question. It's brilliant. And it's part of something that leads into a bigger process called the DOS process. But he likes to say, if we were having this conversation, let's just say you're doing an eight week accelerator. If we were having this conversation eight weeks from today and we were looking back over the past eight weeks, what would have needed to happen in order for you to be happy with the results? So that's one way to ask that question, the Dan Sullivan style. When I ask him that way, I say, hey, have you ever heard of Dan Sullivan? He's got this really cool way to find out what people want to achieve. It goes like this. And then I ask the question because I always believe in giving credit where credit is due. You can also kind of model the way I do it. I have two different ways I'll ask this question. One is I'll say, hey, I usually work with a client for eight weeks. And during that eight week period, we try to hit one big and important target. What's your one big and important target? So that's another way to ask it. And the alternate way I ask it is, OK, what's our mission? What do you want to accomplish together? And actually, this is like the most common thing. And the reason why is because it doesn't really matter how you answer this question. You're going to get these. Not how you answer this question. I'm sorry. How you ask the question. Doesn't matter how you ask it. You're going to get one of these three responses. Response number one is more sales. Great. Thanks. That was helpful. Here's an extra dollar. Happy. So that's like response number one, which is actually not that common. Here's a more common response, which is, well, let me tell you what I'm doing. And then you will get their entire life history and the history of the business and everything that's been going on. And it has nothing to do with what you asked them, by the way. It's absolutely nothing to do with what do you want to accomplish together. And the third response is, excuse me for a minute. Well, I've vomited up all of my problems on you and totally ignore the question that you just asked me. And that's the one where they'll say, so I'll be me and then I'll be them. So this is me. OK, what's our mission? What do you want to accomplish together? Well, man, so I've got this guy and he's been working for me forever. And I just don't know if he's the right person. And our funnel is weird. And I used to get $5 leads, but now I think I don't know. But it feels like they've gone up a lot. And you'll get that for like 15, 20 minutes. So here's the deal. No kidding. And this is why we're actually doing this work together right now. It is very unusual and rare for the client to have a specific answer to that question. And it is by no means because the client is dumb. It's because the client is busy running a successful freaking business. So they can't see the forest through the trees. They're too busy putting out fires. They're either putting out fires or they're worried about future fires based on the past. So very rarely are they ever able to take a minute and actually think, OK, what do I actually want? Which means you cannot accept any of these answers. None of these are acceptable to you. If you accept them, you will fail because you will not be able to perform a diagnosis and prescribe a one-step action plan because there is no target to hit. So it's really, really important. And I know it's like, seriously, dude, you're spending all this time telling me to get someone to answer a question about what specific result do you want to get? And the answer is yes, because you're about to find out through practice that they ain't going to know. They're not going to tell you. All right? They're going to kind of hint, but they're not going to tell you. So here's how you dig. So let's say the response is, well, dude, I just want to make more sales. Well, that's like the best possible response you can get because now your answer can be something like, cool, OK, well, let's talk about that. How much sales volume did you do over the past 90 days or whatever? And they'll answer. If they don't answer, guess what? It's impossible for us to hit this number because we don't have a baseline. Right? So they're like, I don't know. You're like, cool, nobody ever does. Let's find out together. What do you use? Do you use infusion soft? Do you use QuickBooks? What do we need to do to get that answer to this question? You know, we're like right now. And guess what? They're going to be very cooperative because they are your clients. All right? So all we're doing is helping them make more money. All right? So it's pretty rare that actually, when I say pretty rare. It has literally never, ever happened where someone doesn't want to help you find out this data, right? Because the trust has already been established. You're already working with them. You are their trusted advisor. They are your client and they have gone through the entire process that got them to this point. All right? So once you get this answered now, it's real simply, say, cool, OK, how much would you like to do over the next 90 days? All right? And so that's how you do that digging. All right? And there's a little more, and I'll say it kind of to the end here, but that's like phase one of that level digging. Let's talk about the second response. This first one, by the way, rare. Second one, super rare. Well, let me tell you about what I'm doing. And then you just have to, you have got to listen to them, by the way. Don't interrupt them. Let them get it out. Right? And so you wait patiently while they tell you their entire life story. You say nothing. And then finally, when they're done, you know, you say, OK, cool. So what would you like to accomplish together over the next eight weeks? And so you're literally asking the question again, but now you're leading them here. So like, I want you to know this as a script. Great. So what would you like to accomplish together over the next 10 weeks? More sales, more leads, more revenue, or, and I deliberately want you to leave this question open and let them answer. All right? So give them choices and let them pick one. Of course, it's going to be more revenue or more sales. Right? I mean, 90% of the time. But whatever it is, is what it is. It's fine. But leave it open so they can answer. And if they go back into the story, you don't tell them to quit telling you the story. You say, cool, man. All right. Well, OK. And then you just ask this question in another way. All right? So if they didn't, if they went back to the story here, then you might reposition the question and the dance all of them saw. Cool. All right. You know, we're in the future eight weeks from today and we're looking back over the past eight weeks. What's like the one big thing that would have happened in order for you to have considered this to really be a massive success together? And then you keep doing that until they give you an answer. Right? Now, their refusal to give you an answer is not a refusal. Like you're never going to have someone say, I refuse to answer that question. They're already your client. All right. It is going to be, if you find this difficult to get out of them, it's going to be a function of their current state, which is distraction and overwhelm and inability to see forests through trees. So when you're doing this and you're like hounding them here, you're really helping them a lot. It's very, very important. OK? So usually it'll take you a couple passes. You know, like if you got someone that wants to tell you your life story or their life story, you're going to have to ask the question just a few times and phrase it a few ways until you finally get something. Now, let's talk about response number three. All right? And this is super common as well, which is I'm going to ignore your question completely and just vomit up all my problems on you. And that's super normal, by the way, and they're not trying to dodge the question. It's just that they don't have anyone else to tell the problems to, you know? So it's like finally someone's going to listen to me. Let me get this out of my system. Blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah. Now, the empathetic kind of human in you is going to want to start offering solutions to these problems. Do not do it. OK? Don't do it. Most of the time, all the crap that they're worried about, if they just literally stop messing with it, they would make more money. You can't tell them that up front, right? It's too simple for them to process. They're too stuck in problem world. So you just literally let them get it all out. And then you say, great. I know it sounds ridiculous. And I want you to remember this, too, verbatim. Great. Look, man, I know it sounds crazy, but let's just pretend for a minute that all those problems just went away. OK? So with that in mind, what do you want to accomplish together over the next weeks? More sales, more leads, more revenue, and again, leave the question open and let them answer. Now, here's two things are going to happen here. You're actually going to, like, say, more sales, and then you come up here. Oh, cool. OK, more sales. Great. How much sales volume did you do over the past 10 weeks? How much would you like to do over the next, right? Or they are going to go back into the problems and back into the worries. And so your job is to let them do it and then to ask the question again. Cool. All right. So let's pretend all that stuff was solved and then just ask the question from a different angle. Ask it then, solve it and style. Let's pretend all those problems are solved and that, like, we didn't have to worry about any of that stuff. And we've been working together for eight weeks and, like, I just waved the magic wand right now. All those problems went away. If it was eight weeks from now, looking back over the past eight weeks, what would have needed to happen for us to really have been successful? And this would it be more sales or more leads or more revenue and then let them get it out? Right? Here's the deal. Your real mission, no matter what they say here, your real mission is to always establish a baseline and the target and then confirm that with actual numbers. Okay? So here's the example of how to establish a baseline. Okay? They say more sales. So you finally get them to say, okay, I want more sales, right? Which I swear to God, it's, you'll see. Okay? Like, you'll be like, dude, I had no idea it was as hard to get someone's specific objective. So finally, they told you their life history or they told you all their problems. They finally say, you know what I really want is more sales. You're like, great. How much revenue did you do over the past eight weeks and how much would you like to do over the next eight weeks? Okay? And then they tell you, right? And so here's an example of confirming with actual numbers. Let's say they did 100 grand over the past eight weeks. You'd say, so from here, you're right. You did 100 grand over the past eight weeks and you'd like to do 150 over the next eight weeks. Therefore, our mission together is to bring in an additional $50,000. Does that sound right to you? And so what you've got to get is an affirmative yes on this right here that I'm circling with my mouse, right? So you've got to reframe the question and keep asking the question until you get to this actual point. This is a specific target. This is what the state of objective is. You know, I wanted to add this to the end of that lesson because I can already tell psychically I'm able to read the future. I bet you didn't know that. There are going to be some people that go through this. They're like, well, this isn't relevant to me. I'm not a consultant. You know how to fix my business right now. And what I would urge you to do is to pretend like you are giving a consulting session to yourself. I never start a new project without literally sitting by myself in a room. It's actually that room right over there. There's like a regular old chair there with an ottoman. And I sit there and I have a little notepad out and I will actually go through this entire process before beginning any new project. And I will do it quarterly when reviewing the quarter and setting up goals for the upcoming quarter for my own company. And the reason this is so important for you to do and literally like pretend, I know it sounds dull. I were a couple of adults talking about pretending, you know, but literally pretend like you are a consultant talking to yourself. Now, if someone's watching you, you're going to look like you're crazy, but maybe don't do it with anybody watching. Not something you want to do in Starbucks, you know, but if you do this, it forces your brain to get out of the weeds of the business and actually look at your business from a higher level. And I can promise you, especially when you do what I'm about to show you how to do in this next section, what you're going to find out if you'll just stick with me through this like somewhat cerebral part here is there's tons of opportunity that you've already got and you're probably not taking advantage of it. So the reason I'm walking you through this, even though, you know, you might have signed up for the certification because you want to get more clients for your business. You don't care about like doing consulting or anything. It's because this is the process that you need to take yourself through. All right. So instead of paying someone else a lot of money to ultimately do this for you, you just do it for yourself. Okay. Your mission, all right, is to do this on your own business. So literally, you know, as weird as it sounds, you don't actually have to do it out loud or anything, but go through this entire process for your business as if, you know, this side of you is the consultant and this side of you is the business owner and you're just getting all of this data. All right. And then writing it down because this is the beginning of the raw material to you getting money like really, really quickly, which I know is what you want and that's what I want you to have. But it's going to be very difficult to get it for you without doing these steps. Cool. Okay. So follow that mission. I'll see you in the next video.
